reasoning: The base rate for any specific non-president winning TIME's Person of the Year is very low (around 1-3%). Elon Musk has already won the award in 2021; historically, TIME has never awarded Person of the Year twice to a businessperson or private citizen (only to US Presidents, and a few historic world leaders). Furthermore, for 2025, there are countless potential candidates, movements, and global events that could take center stage. While Musk remains highly influential through his companies and his role in the proposed Department of Government Efficiency, the bar for a repeat selection is incredibly high. Thus, a probability of 3% is appropriate.
